lyrics:
War and peace
Profit and poverty
Open and closed
Slave and free
Covert and overt
Fear and trust
Loud and still
Ash and dust

They don’t collide, they intertwine-
Into the outer
Opposites attract in time

Love and hate
Push and pull
Tender and sharp
Empty and full
Sun and shadow
Rise and fall
Silk and iron
Nothing and all

They twist, they tangle, never match-
Into the outer
But opposites attract

Truth and lie
High and low
Fast and slow
Yes and no
Order and chaos
Knife and thread
Breath and silence
Living and dead

Not black and white, but every crack-
Into the outer
Where opposites attract

Sacred and broken
Flesh and flame
Known and nameless
Pride and shame
Stone and water
Grace and lack
What pulls apart
Still circles back

The tether holds, the lines collapse-
Into the outer
When opposites attract

Into the outer
When opposites attract
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track creates a vast, shimmering texture with layers of arpeggiated clean guitars and a wall of distorted power chords, establishing a powerful atmosphere that is both cathartic and emotionally yearning. The energy is consistently high and driving, propelled by a forceful rhythm section that gives the song a full-bodied, anthemic feel. The production is remarkably wide and clear for its density, allowing the various guitar parts and the airy, reverb-soaked vocals to occupy their own distinct space. A defining quality of the track is its explosive, open-sounding choruses, where the music swells into a massive soundscape, providing moments of intense emotional release that anchor the song's journey.

review.review_text: Running in 3/4 at a brisk 117 BPM, 'Opposites Attract' finds Lingua Aeternum in full anthem mode: arpeggiated clean guitars build the verse with patience, then the chorus detonates into wide, distorted power chords that genuinely earn the release. The lyric pairs are almost liturgical in their sweep, 'breath and silence, living and dead,' and the waltz feel keeps that catalog of dualities from feeling like a list, giving each line a small, rolling momentum. The production is smart enough to keep the vocal reverb airy even when the guitars are stacked thick, so the climax breathes where a lesser mix would just compress. A highlight.
anchors_used: arpeggiated clean guitars vs. distorted power chord choruses
lyric: 'breath and silence, living and dead'
